summary: The PoC demonstrates that agent HTTP endpoints accept unauthenticated requests and execute queries with full tool access. An unauthenticated HTTP POST request triggers tool execution that creates a proof-of-concept marker file, confirming complete absence of authentication.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should create a marker file at /tmp/http_pwned via unauthenticated HTTP POST request to the agent endpoint, demonstrating that attackers can execute any tool or query without authentication.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh launched an agent HTTP server, sent an unauthenticated POST request to the endpoint, and the agent executed a tool that created /tmp/http_pwned, confirming missing authentication on HTTP endpoints.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of /tmp/http_pwned after sending an unauthenticated HTTP request. File existence confirms the agent executed the tool without any authentication, distinguishing real authorization bypass from network errors or server startup failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: Marker file /tmp/http_pwned created via unauthenticated HTTP POST request
No authentication headers, API keys, or session tokens required
Agent executed tool with full capabilities despite missing auth
HTTP endpoint accessible on network interface (0.0.0.0)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
